records.label: Runtime component tests
records.writes: temporary files only where component tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A runtime seam or engine/gateway/connector contract regressed.
records.notes: Primary Python reference-backend correctness lane.

records.label: Surface tests
records.writes: temporary files only where surface tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A web, CLI, or API projection regressed.
records.notes: Surfaces must stay on the public side of the architecture.

records.label: Repo-level tests
records.writes: temporary files only where integration tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A cross-component, operations, eval, architecture, or parity guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Includes architecture, integration, operations, eval, and parity validation.

records.label: Architecture-boundary checker
records.failure_means: A Python import crossed an accepted runtime/gateway/surface/connectors boundary.
records.notes: Narrow repo-local import guardrail, not a universal policy engine.

records.label: Public-alpha smoke checks
records.failure_means: The constrained public-alpha allowed/blocked posture regressed.
records.notes: Does not deploy or expose a service.

records.label: Public-alpha hosting pack summary check
records.failure_means: The hosting pack route-safety summary drifted from the route inventory.
records.notes: Documentation consistency check only.

records.label: Python-oracle golden fixture check
records.failure_means: Committed Python oracle golden outputs drifted from current reference behavior.
records.notes: Required for Python oracle and future Rust parity discipline.

records.label: Archive-resolution eval runner
records.writes: temporary local index unless explicit output path is provided
records.failure_means: The hard-query archive-resolution guardrail no longer executes or reports honestly.
records.notes: Not a ranking, fuzzy, vector, LLM, crawling, or production relevance benchmark.

records.label: Search usefulness audit runner
records.writes: temporary local index unless explicit output path is provided
records.failure_means: The broad usefulness audit no longer produces local Eureka observations and pending manual baselines.
records.notes: External baselines must remain pending unless a human records observations.

records.label: Public-alpha route inventory validation
records.failure_means: Public-alpha route inventory structure or local-path classifications regressed.
records.notes: Pairs with public-alpha smoke checks.

records.label: Source registry runtime tests
records.failure_means: Source Registry v0 loading, validation, or filtering regressed.
records.notes: Keeps governed source inventory records coherent.

records.label: Source coverage and capability model tests
records.writes: temporary files only for malformed-record checks
records.failure_means: Source capability flags, coverage-depth values, or placeholder-source honesty regressed.
records.notes: Added by Source Coverage and Capability Model v0; it does not add connectors, live probes, or crawling.

records.label: Test/eval operating layer validation
records.failure_means: The test registry, command matrix, or AIDE queue stopped being machine-readable.
records.notes: Added by Comprehensive Test/Eval Operating Layer and Repo Audit v0.

records.label: Comprehensive audit pack validation
records.failure_means: The comprehensive audit pack lost required files, structured findings, or anti-fake-success guardrails.
records.notes: Validates the repo-native audit outputs from this milestone.

records.label: Search usefulness backlog triage validation
records.failure_means: The search usefulness triage pack lost required docs, selected wedges, selected next milestone, or anti-fake-success guardrails.
records.notes: Added by Search Usefulness Backlog Triage v0; validates governance/backlog artifacts only.

records.label: Hard Test Pack v0 aggregate
records.writes: temporary files only where called scripts create temp stores
records.failure_means: A high-risk eval truth, path-safety, drift, parity, source honesty, memory privacy, or repo-operating guard regressed.
records.notes: Added by Hard Test Pack v0; no product behavior, external scraping, or production-readiness claims.

records.label: Hard eval weakening guard
records.writes: temporary local index only through eval/audit scripts
records.failure_means: Hard archive-resolution or search-usefulness fixtures were weakened, removed, or stopped reporting current hard gaps honestly.
records.notes: Uses minimum thresholds and required IDs so adding harder queries remains allowed.

records.label: External baseline fabrication guard
records.failure_means: Google or Internet Archive baselines may have been marked observed without manual evidence, or docs/scripts imply scraping.
records.notes: Does not perform any external lookup.

records.label: Public-alpha path leakage guard
records.failure_means: Public-alpha status or blocked responses exposed private local path values or route inventory marked local-path routes safe.
records.notes: Uses sentinel paths and checks names, not values, are returned.

records.label: Route inventory drift guard
records.failure_means: The public-alpha route inventory drifted from known web/API routes or route-policy blocked constants.
records.notes: Uses bounded static checks and explicit exemptions.

records.label: README command drift guard
records.writes: temporary local index or fixture output only through safe commands
records.failure_means: README links or representative commands drifted from real repo paths/scripts.
records.notes: Avoids persistent server commands.

records.label: Documentation link drift guard
records.failure_means: A major local Markdown link no longer resolves.
records.notes: External URLs and generated anchors are intentionally out of scope.

records.label: Python oracle golden drift guard
records.failure_means: Python-oracle goldens drifted, required families disappeared, or private paths leaked into committed goldens.
records.notes: Delegates detailed parity fixture checks to the existing generator.

records.label: Rust parity structure guard
records.failure_means: Rust parity scaffolding drifted, was wired into Python runtime, or docs started implying active production Rust behavior.
records.notes: Does not require Cargo.

records.label: Source placeholder honesty guard
records.failure_means: Placeholder/future source records or public projections may be claiming implemented behavior.
records.notes: Keeps source inventory honesty distinct from future connector work.

records.label: Resolution memory privacy/path guard
records.failure_means: Resolution memory docs/goldens/audit outputs may leak private roots or imply cloud/shared/user-tracking behavior.
records.notes: A bounded privacy/path guard, not a complete privacy audit.

records.label: AIDE/test registry consistency guard
records.failure_means: AIDE queue/report scaffolding, test registry, or command matrix references drifted from one another.
records.notes: Does not require a local AIDE runtime.

records.label: Whitespace diff check
records.failure_means: Whitespace or conflict-marker issues are present in the working diff.
records.notes: Does not validate links; future docs drift guard should cover that.

records.label: Optional Rust workspace check
records.failure_means: The optional Rust skeleton or candidate seam does not compile in an environment with Cargo.
records.notes: Must not be required for normal Python verification while Cargo availability varies.

records.label: Optional Rust workspace tests
records.failure_means: Optional Rust parity tests failed in an environment with Cargo.
records.notes: Optional until the repo explicitly requires a Rust toolchain.
